commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is maintainer compatibility acceptance plus normal required-check handling for the PR head.; Cleared: The diff only changes daemon service adapter behavior and focused tests; it does not touch CI, dependencies, permissions, package resolution, or secret handling. (sha=c33f9f05a78b8058b31991e23eea2a8fe2a7677d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes unsupported Gateway service resolution to return a read-only adapter, keeps status rendering readable with an unknown runtime detail, makes unsupported lifecycle checks reject before fallback handling, and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +27, Tests +146. Total +173 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main throws from resolveGatewayService() on unsupported platforms before status callers can render service details; the PR's terminal proof and tests exercise the same branch by stubbing process.platform.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is maintainer compatibility acceptance plus normal required-check handling for the PR head.

target.title: fix(daemon): keep status readable on unsupported services
target.body:
## Summary
- Keep unsupported Gateway service lifecycle commands rejecting at the service-check boundary, before not-loaded recovery or unmanaged stop/restart fallback can run.
- Preserve read-only status behavior for unsupported service adapters by keeping runtime/status rendering degraded but readable.
- Add focused lifecycle-core regression coverage for unsupported `start`, `stop`, and `restart` paths.

Fixes #25621

## Root cause and fix classification
- Root cause: The unsupported Gateway service adapter reported `isLoaded=false`, so lifecycle-core treated unsupported platforms as ordinary not-loaded services before it reached mutating adapter methods that still rejected. That broke the lifecycle contract for unsupported platforms and allowed start/stop/restart paths to enter not-loaded or unmanaged fallback handling instead of surfacing the platform unsupported error at the service-check boundary.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The patch changes the unsupported adapter source boundary itself: `isLoaded()` now rejects with the same unsupported-platform error used by mutating lifecycle methods, so lifecycle-core never misclassifies an unsupported platform as a normal unloaded or unmanaged service. Read-only status still uses the adapter runtime detail path, which keeps the original status-readability fix intact rather than masking the lifecycle bug downstream.
- Fix classification: Root-cause fix for unsupported Gateway service lifecycle contract handling.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High for the local-code regression scope. The remaining live non-Linux host gap is disclosed below and not claimed as tested.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: Unsupported Gateway service platforms keep status readable, but mutating lifecycle operations reject before not-loaded recovery or unmanaged fallback handling.
- Real environment tested: Local Linux worktree at `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91626`, using the production daemon service resolver and lifecycle-core command paths. The runtime proof executes those production modules from a terminal and stubs `process.platform` to `aix` only to reach the unsupported-platform adapter branch from this Linux host; no live non-Linux host is claimed.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false corepack pnpm@11.2.2 exec vitest run src/daemon/service.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle-core.test.ts src/commands/status.daemon.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91626 exec node --import tsx /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91626-evidence/unsupported-lifecycle-runtime-proof.mjs
```
- Evidence after fix:
```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91626

Test Files  4 passed (4)
     Tests  71 passed | 1 skipped (72)
  Start at  18:23:44
  Duration  7.20s (transform 2.77s, setup 1.13s, import 2.90s, tests 2.42s, environment 1ms)

proof=unsupported-gateway-lifecycle-runtime
platform=aix
service.label=Gateway service
status.loaded=false
status.loadedText=not installed
status.runtime={"status":"unknown","detail":"Gateway service install not supported on aix"}
{
  "action": "start",
  "ok": false,
  "error": "Gateway service check failed: Error: Gateway service install not supported on aix"
}
start.caught=__exit__:1
start.onNotLoadedCalled=false
{
  "action": "stop",
  "ok": false,
  "error": "Gateway service check failed: Error: Gateway service install not supported on aix"
}
stop.caught=__exit__:1
stop.onNotLoadedCalled=false
{
  "action": "restart",
  "ok": false,
  "error": "Gateway service check failed: Error: Gateway service install not supported on aix"
}
restart.caught=__exit__:1
restart.onNotLoadedCalled=false
restart.postRestartCheckCalled=false
```
- Observed result after fix: The focused test run passes. The terminal runtime proof shows status remains readable with `runtime.status="unknown"` and the unsupported-platform detail, while production lifecycle `start`, `stop`, and `restart` emit the unsupported service-check error and do not call not-loaded recovery or unmanaged restart follow-up handlers.
- What was not tested: No live AIX/OpenBSD/non-Linux host was available in this Linux environment, and no live Gateway service install/start/stop was attempted on an unsupported OS. Local chat/RPC/Control UI proof on Linux would exercise the supported Linux adapter, not the unsupported adapter/lifecycle branch requested by RF-002/RF-006, so it would not prove this reviewer blocker.

## Regression test plan
- Target test file: `src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle-core.test.ts`, `src/daemon/service.test.ts`, `src/commands/status.daemon.test.ts`, and `src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: Unsupported-platform `start`, `stop`, and `restart` now reject during the lifecycle service check, and unsupported read-only status still reports a readable `unknown` runtime detail instead of throwing through the status command.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The tests run the production resolver and lifecycle-core command boundaries that previously misclassified the unsupported adapter, so the regression guard covers the exact lifecycle invariant without adding unrelated platform fixtures or broad service-manager behavior.

## Scope and architecture boundaries
- What did NOT change: This does not change Linux, macOS, or Windows service manager implementations; it does not add a new installation path; it does not change Gateway runtime startup behavior; it only changes the unsupported adapter load-check contract and the focused tests around that contract.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: `src/daemon/service.ts` remains the source-of-truth for Gateway service platform adapters. The lifecycle-core code continues to consume the resolved adapter contract instead of adding a downstream special case for unsupported platforms.

## Review findings addressed
- RF-001: The PR was in `status: waiting on author`; this update addresses the current author-side blocker and provides current-head local evidence for re-review.
- RF-002: Unsupported lifecycle commands now reject before not-loaded or unmanaged fallback handling because the unsupported adapter load check rejects.
- RF-003: Added focused lifecycle-core regression coverage for unsupported `start`, `stop`, and `restart` paths.
- RF-004: The incorrect behavior described by ClawSweeper is covered by tests that verify no not-loaded recovery or unmanaged fallback output is used for unsupported lifecycle commands.
- RF-005: The repair is intentionally narrow at the unsupported adapter/lifecycle boundary plus focused regression tests; no broader service refactor was added.
- RF-006: `src/daemon/service.ts` now keeps unsupported lifecycle checks rejecting through `isLoaded()` as well as mutating methods.
- RF-007: Focused service/status/lifecycle Vitest coverage was run after the patch on the current head.
- RF-008: The daily-fix commit/verification flow ran after formatting; the submitted diff has a fresh verification marker for the current head.
- RF-009: Formatting ran during the daily-fix commit flow, then the focused tests and verification marker were refreshed on the formatted current head.

## Merge risk
- Risk labels considered: `merge-risk: compatibility`, `merge-risk: availability`, `merge-risk: automation`, `merge-risk: message-delivery`, and `merge-risk: session-state`.
- Risk explanation: Compatibility risk is limited to unsupported platforms, where mutating Gateway service lifecycle commands already cannot succeed. Availability and message-delivery risk are lowered because unsupported lifecycle paths now fail explicitly instead of implying a successful not-loaded or unmanaged fallback state. Automation/session-state signals come from lifecycle tests and status gathering, not from changes to session persistence or automation infrastructure.
- Why acceptable: The change narrows behavior to the documented unsupported-platform lifecycle contract while preserving the previous PR's readable status behavior. Supported service adapters keep their existing platform-specific implementations, and the regression tests cover both the mutating lifecycle rejection path and the read-only status path.
- Patch quality notes: The patch-quality warnings about the word `fallback` come from regression test names that assert unsupported stop/restart reject before unmanaged fallback; they are not new production fallback behavior. The merge-risk warnings are addressed above, and the source change stays in the adapter source-of-truth rather than masking the issue with downstream guards.

## Maintainer-ready assessment
- Why it matters / User impact: Users on unsupported platforms should see a clear unsupported Gateway service error for mutating lifecycle commands instead of misleading install hints or unmanaged fallback behavior. That makes CLI output safer to interpret and keeps status readable without suggesting unsupported lifecycle operations worked.
